title: Success Criteria, Baselines, and Prompt Evals · Guided Eval Studio
summary: Define and test a support-triage prompt before optimizing its wording. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of a versioned prompt evaluation harness while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define the user, task, decision, acceptable output, and material failure modes before drafting a prompt.
Turn subjective quality goals into observable criteria and representative test cases.
Create a minimal baseline prompt before adding scaffolding or examples.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]

Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]

steps.title: Defend the transferred model

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine deterministic checks, rubric review, and human inspection without treating one judge as truth.
